title: CryptiQ glossary term
description: One post-quantum cryptography glossary entry. Rendered by src/pages/GlossaryTerm.jsx and prerendered to static HTML by scripts/prerender.mjs. Validated by scripts/validate-content.mjs before every build.

slug.description: URL segment under /glossary/. Must equal the filename without .json.

term.description: Canonical display name. Use final NIST names (ML-KEM, ML-DSA, SLH-DSA) — never CRYSTALS-* as the primary name.

acronym.description: Spelled-out form when the term is an abbreviation (e.g. 'Module-Lattice-Based Key-Encapsulation Mechanism').

aliases.description: Former or alternate names (e.g. CRYSTALS-Kyber). Used for schema.org alternateName.

shortDefinition.description: One-sentence definition. Doubles as the meta description — front-load the term and keep under 165 chars.

definition.description: Lead paragraphs. First sentence of the first paragraph must define the term outright.

faq.description: Real questions practitioners ask. Emitted as FAQPage JSON-LD.

related.description: Slugs of other glossary entries. Every slug must exist — enforced by the validator.

sources.description: Authoritative references only (NIST, CISA, NSA, IETF, vendor engineering blogs). Real URLs — verified during review.

cryptiqAngle.description: How the term connects to cryptographic inventory / CBOM / migration work. Factual tone, not sales copy.
